entity.summary: Hong Kong-linked centralized cryptocurrency exchange founded around 2017. BitForex became inaccessible and halted withdrawals in February 2024, was placed on Hong Kong's suspicious-platform alert list in March 2024, and later reopened only an asset-withdrawal system. As of April 2026, the platform reported continued withdrawal processing while trading and deposits remained unavailable.
entity.notes: HEI retains limited rather than dead because BitForex currently operates a withdrawal-only asset-recovery system and publishes periodic processing updates. This is not normal exchange operation: trading and deposits remain unavailable, and the Hong Kong SFC warning remains material. A terminal death date should not be assigned while withdrawal processing continues.

expected.summary: Hong Kong-linked centralized cryptocurrency exchange founded around 2017. BitForex halted withdrawals and became inaccessible in February 2024 after large wallet outflows and alleged police detention of team members. Hong Kong's SFC warned the public about suspected virtual asset fraud involving BitForex in March 2024. BitForex later said it would reopen only for withdrawals, with trading and deposits remaining unavailable.
expected.notes: HEI uses limited rather than dead because BitForex's own current notice says withdrawal processing resumed after July 24, 2024, while other reporting says trading and deposits remain suspended and all services are to cease after withdrawals. death_reason is null because this is not classified as a terminal dead-side record yet.

changes.summary: Hong Kong-linked centralized cryptocurrency exchange founded around 2017. BitForex became inaccessible and halted withdrawals in February 2024, was placed on Hong Kong's suspicious-platform alert list in March 2024, and later reopened only an asset-withdrawal system. As of April 2026, the platform reported continued withdrawal processing while trading and deposits remained unavailable.
changes.notes: HEI retains limited rather than dead because BitForex currently operates a withdrawal-only asset-recovery system and publishes periodic processing updates. This is not normal exchange operation: trading and deposits remain unavailable, and the Hong Kong SFC warning remains material. A terminal death date should not be assigned while withdrawal processing continues.

evidence.title: SFC warns public of BitForex for suspected virtual asset-related fraud
evidence.publisher: Securities and Futures Commission of Hong Kong
evidence.notes: The SFC warning records withdrawal difficulties, website inaccessibility, lack of licensing, and placement on the suspicious VATP alert list.

evidence.title: BitForex Asset Withdrawal System monthly announcement
evidence.notes: Current withdrawal-only portal states that processing resumed after July 24, 2024 and reports ongoing withdrawals through April 2026; it does not show restoration of trading or deposits.
